steps.title: Identify the vertex from the graph
steps.content: The parabola's vertex (minimum point) is at coordinates $(0, 3)$.

steps.title: Match using vertex form
steps.content:
In vertex form $y = a(x - h)^2 + k$, the vertex is at $(h, k)$. Writing each choice in vertex form:
  • A: $5(x + 1)^2 + 2$ → vertex at $(-1, 2)$
  • B: $5(x + 1)^2 + 0$ → vertex at $(-1, 0)$
  • C: $5x^2 + 3 = 5(x - 0)^2 + 3$ → vertex at $(0, 3)$
  • D: $5x^2 + 0 = 5(x - 0)^2$ → vertex at $(0, 0)$
Only choice C has vertex $(0, 3)$, matching the graph shown. That matches choice C — C is correct.
